2604.16117.title: SCRIPT: Implementing an Intelligent Tutoring System for Programming in a German University Context
2604.16117.paper_summary: Practice and extensive exercises are essential in programming education. Intelligent tutoring systems (ITSs) are a viable option to provide individualized hints and advice to programming students even when human tutors are not available. However, prior ITS for programming rarely support the Python programming language, mostly focus on introductory programming, and rarely take recent developments in generative models into account. We aim to establish a novel ITS for Python programming that is highly adaptable, serves both as a teaching and research platform, provides interfaces to plug in hint mechanisms (e.g.\ via large language models), and works inside the particularly challenging regulatory environment of Germany, that is, conforming to the European data protection regulation, the European AI act, and ethical framework of the German Research Foundation. In this paper, we present the description of the current state of the ITS along with future development directions, as well as discuss the challenges and opportunities for improving the system.
2604.16117.paper_summary_zh: 實踐和廣泛的練習在程式設計教育中是至關重要的。智能輔導系統（ITS）是一個可行的選擇，可以在沒有人類輔導員的情況下，為程式設計學生提供個性化的提示和建議。然而，先前的程式設計 ITS 很少支持 Python 程式語言，主要集中於入門程式設計，並且很少考慮生成模型的最新發展。我們的目標是建立一個新穎的 Python 程式設計 ITS，該系統高度適應性，既作為教學平台也作為研究平台，提供接口以插入提示機制（例如通過大型語言模型），並在德國特別具挑戰性的監管環境中運作，即符合歐洲數據保護法規、歐洲人工智慧法案以及德國研究基金會的倫理框架。在本文中，我們介紹了該 ITS 的當前狀態及未來發展方向，並討論了改善該系統的挑戰和機會。
2604.16117.author: Alina Deriyeva et.al.
2604.16117.authors: Alina Deriyeva, Jesper Dannath, Benjamin Paassen
